characters.name: Bill Clinton
characters.description: Bill Clinton was the 43rd President of the United States of America from 1993-2001. In theAnimaniacs-Pinky and the BrainUniverse, President Clinton was seen as a somewhat fun-loving country-bumpkin who had a strong hankering for Pizza. Despite being thePresident of the U.S., he usually deferred most of his important responsibilities to his wifeHillary Clinton, essentially making him a figurehead while making her the de facto head of Government. During the 1996 U.S. Presidential Campaign, Democratic Candidate Bill Clinton (along with opponent Republican Candidate Bob Dole) debated and lost to (Third Party) Pink Candidate Pinky, however after President Pinky resigned from Office (following hiscampaign manager's Congressional Inquest), Clinton was somehow reappointed to the Office of President.

characters.name: Billie
characters.description: BillieVoiceTress MacNeilleSpeciesLaboratory mouseBody colorWhite with pink ears, face, chest, and tail. Red noseEye colorBlue with sky-blue scleraeGenderFemaleHomeACME Labs  Billieis a female mouse, another result of gene splicing. She is smarter thanBrain, and makes him afraid she will beat him to his goal of world domination. Brain loves her, however, Billie fanciesPinky. Despite Billie having great intelligence, she can act a bit ditzy at times. Billie first appeared on Animaniacs in the short "The World Can Wait".

characters.name: Billy West
characters.description: Billy West  William Richard "Billy" Werstineis an American voice actor, best known for his numerous voice roles such as the titular characters Ren Höek (after John Kricfalusi's dismisal) and Stimpy in the Nickelodeon seriesThe Ren & Stimpy Showfrom 1991 to 1996; and Philip J. Fry, Hubert J. Farnsworth, Zapp Brannigan and Dr. John A. Zoidberg in the Fox sci-fi sitcomFuturamafrom 1999 to 2026 and one of the current voices of Elmer Fudd and occasionally Bugs Bunny in variousLooney Tunesincarnations (though he would switch with fellow voice actors, such as Jeff Bergman).

characters.name: Brain
characters.description: "The same thing we do every night, Pinky. Try to take over the world!" -Brain toPinky Species Brain is rather sarcastic and grumpy at times, but he has a soft, and tender heart. He at times seems emotionless and uncaring but shows sides of affection and care and doesn't go out of his way to screw over others in his quest for domination. He seems to simply believe he is the best at everything, of superior intelligence, and that he deserves to rule the world. He often comments on how great and intelligent he is, and enjoys an audience, as many of his attempts to take over the world involve putting on some type of show or becoming well known and famous. In Brain's Song[1]he admits he has "detached from his emotions," and tries to get Pinky to do so as well by showing him sad films and training him not to cry. He refers to emotions as "the bane of our existence." However soon after, he ends up crying alongside Pinky from empathy towards the character in the sad film. He has shown an intention to be a benevolent world leader when he agrees to Pinky that the Constitution is good to follow, but that of course he is allowed to temporarily break it in order to get into power. He also genuinely enjoys playing different acting roles while trying to trick and manipulate the world into giving him power, and he gets Pinky to dress up and be an accomplice in the act. He has a soft spot for Pinky although he often gets aggravated at Pinky and says he's going to have to hurt him. Pinky and Brain have a close bond that even borders on romantic, although Brain isn't one to show emotion and/or affection. Brain was based off of famous actorsOrson WellesandVincent Price.

characters.name: Charlie Sheen
characters.description: Charlie Sheen is an actor who takes drugs and used to be on Two and a Half men.  He is mentioned in the Animaniacs segmentSpellbound, in a spell Brain used throughout the episode.  He is later mentioned by by Pinky, when Brain says that they are going to a place where females are paid to change constanly, in the episodeCalvin Brain.  Pinky responds by asking whether this place is Charlie Sheen's House.
